title: Bite Back Against Rabies Day
story: Every September 28, people around the world unite for World Rabies Day to spread awareness about preventing this serious disease. Veterinarians offer special vaccination clinics for pets, while schools and communities host educational events teaching families how to stay safe around wild animals. Social media fills with photos of freshly vaccinated dogs and cats wearing special bandanas or stickers.
activity_question: What do veterinarians offer on World Rabies Day?
follow_up_question: Why do you think pets need rabies vaccines to stay healthy?

title: Sweet Dreams Are Made of Cream Pie
story: Every September 28, pie lovers across America celebrate National Strawberry Cream Pie Day by baking and eating this delicious dessert! This special day honors the perfect combination of sweet strawberries, fluffy whipped cream, and buttery pie crust. Bakeries often feature strawberry cream pies as their daily special, while families gather in kitchens to make homemade versions together. Some people share photos of their colorful creations on social media, showing off perfectly arranged strawberry slices on top of billowy cream. Whether you buy one from your favorite bakery or make it from scratch, this holiday gives everyone a sweet reason to indulge in one of summer's most beloved flavors.
activity_question: What are the three main ingredients that make strawberry cream pie special?
activity_choices: Strawberries, whipped cream, and pie crust
Bananas, peanut butter, and graham crackers
activity_answer: Strawberries, whipped cream, and pie crust
bonus_fact: The first strawberry cream pie recipes appeared in American cookbooks in the 1800s!

title: Ask a Stupid Question Day Gets Smart
story: Every September 28, teachers and students celebrate Ask a Stupid Question Day by encouraging everyone to ask ANY question without feeling embarrassed! This fun holiday started because teachers noticed kids were afraid to ask questions they thought might sound dumb. On this day, classrooms buzz with curious questions like "Why don't we fall off the Earth?" and "Can fish get thirsty?" Teachers love it because they know there's no such thing as a truly stupid question - only curious minds wanting to learn. Many schools hold special Q&A sessions where students can ask anything they've always wondered about.
activity_question: Why was Ask a Stupid Question Day created?
activity_choices: Because kids were afraid to ask questions they thought sounded dumb
Because teachers ran out of things to teach
Because students were asking too many smart questions
Because schools needed a new holiday to celebrate
activity_answer: Because kids were afraid to ask questions they thought sounded dumb
follow_up_question: What's a question you've always wanted to ask but felt too shy to?

title: Wisdom Day for the Ancient Teacher
story: September 28 marks Confucius Day, honoring the brilliant Chinese teacher who lived over 2,500 years ago! Schools around the world celebrate by sharing his famous sayings about kindness, respect, and learning. Students might bow to show respect, light incense, or write their favorite wise quotes on colorful paper. Many people post his teachings on social media too. It's a perfect day to remember that wisdom never gets old!
activity_question: How do students often show respect on Confucius Day?
follow_up_question: What wise saying would you want people to remember about you?
